title: factory block + bindings + identity targets
manager: The factory block — parameterized construction where a `required` val is an unfilled parameter and the factory block fills it — with bindings that can read earlier bindings and identity targets (instance_name, machine_name, start_at, seed). This is how FSL builds many configured instances from one template: the factory is the constructor, required vals are its typed parameters, and the checker won't let you forget one.
cs: one `factory{}` per doc; bindings read earlier bindings; identity targets instance_name/machine_name/start_at/seed; `seq()`/`rand(lo,hi)` builtins; fsl#413/#430/#431/#443/#459.
